question: Judge blindness (JD-1): is holdout-in-packet a code defect, or docs-only given EVAL-2-D002?
author: claude/review

rationale: Delegated by human ('on the side of deterministic and untainted judgment'); resolved by claude/opus, vetoable. Identity-blinding (already enforced, verified sound) is what keeps judgment untainted; holdout results are outcome data, not identity data, and are the judge's spec'd input (EVAL-2-D002). Only consequence is judge_preference correlated with holdout_pass_rate by design -> analysis-side disclosure (Phase 5), plus master-plan wording fix. No packet change.
author: jyang (delegated to claude/opus)

question: Official findings.json process section (AN-12): strip, or keep labeled?
author: claude/review

rationale: Retain the process section in official findings.json under an explicit advisory/process label per EVAL-9 AC-6; findings_sha256 keeps covering it (no hash-contract break); make the official markdown show it labeled too. Implementation: Phase 5.
author: jyang

question: Verdict confidence (JD-12): migrate code to spec enum low|medium|high, or amend spec to float?
author: claude/review

rationale: Migrate the verdict schema to low|medium|high per the pre-registered spec; stop discarding the parsed value for a hardcoded 0.8/0.5. Versioned event-schema change with a compatibility note. Implementation: Phase 5.
author: jyang

question: Degenerate kappa (JD-4): return 1.0/sufficient or undefined/insufficient at zero chance-corrected information?
author: claude/review

question: Task-content commitment (PL-7/GR-5): hash tasks.yaml into the lock, or accelerate the corpus-manifest path?
author: claude/review

rationale: Accelerate the corpus-manifest path, scoped for Phase 1: pin {corpus_id, semver, sha256(per-task shas)} into experiment_locked (additive field); run/grade recompute and fail closed on a post-lock swap; fake_holdout_output removed from the production grade path. Full manifest+cache-as-source (holdout import into the cache, is_schedulable at run) deferred to Phase 4 because the cache does not yet store holdouts. Implemented in harness/corpus/commit.py + plan/run/grade wiring.
author: jyang

question: Python 3.11 floor vs plan's 3.12+ (XC-6): intentional debt, or add a 3.12 gate?
author: claude/review

rationale: Confirmed by jyang at Phase-2 start. Key the flake quarantine by task version (task_id, task_sha) per EVAL-5 AC-2 ('quarantines that task version ... ledgered with the task sha'); a clean baseline for a new task version no longer clears an old version's quarantine. Blast radius: load_quarantine returns (task_id,task_sha) pairs; the run-side Task carries task_sha from corpus.commit.task_content_sha; the scheduler checks the pair; test_ac2_new_clean_baseline_clears_quarantine is rewritten (same-sha clean re-baseline clears, different-sha does not) with sign-off. Implementation: Phase 2 slice 2D.
author: jyang

question: Trial-image contract (RN-4): how do prompt + arm config reach the container? Harbor delivers neither today, so a real trial cannot know its task and the arms are indistinguishable inside.
author: claude/review

rationale: Confirmed by jyang at Phase-2 start. The harness writes {prompt,model,arm,payload} to a host temp file and bind-mounts it read-only at /verdi/request.json, outside /workspace; the pre-baked image entrypoint reads it. Keeps the request off the argv/docker-inspect and off the graded workspace copy, agent cannot mutate it, mirrors the holdouts-ro grading pattern. New harness<->image public seam: documented and stable. Implementation: Phase 2 slice 2F.
author: jyang

question: Operational run-config source (RN-13): where do proxy/keys/quotas come from? Keys must never enter the sha-locked experiment.yaml or the ledger; the real fork is where pinned quotas live.
author: claude/review

rationale: Confirmed by jyang at Phase-2 start. Proxy/allowlist/quotas resolve from a run-config file (or CLI flags) at bench run; provider-key VALUES come from the process env by name and are never persisted to spec or ledger [AC-8]. Quotas are pinned identically per arm [D003] and recorded in provenance [AC-6]; they stay out of the pre-registered locked experiment.yaml. Implementation: Phase 2 slice 2E.
author: jyang

question: Metering-proxy attribution + log format (RN-11): how does the proxy attribute egress to a trial? Detection parses only the fake engine's string today and nothing creates/verifies the verdi-metered network.
author: claude/review

rationale: Confirmed by jyang at Phase-2 start. The harness injects the trial id as a per-trial proxy-auth credential so the metering proxy stamps every structured-JSONL log line with the trial; the verdi-metered docker network is created/verified to reach only the proxy; _scan_proxy_log parses the JSONL keyed on trial; the fake engine emits the same JSONL so one parser serves both engines. Per-trial network+proxy isolation noted as future hardening. Implementation: Phase 2 slices 2G/2H.
author: jyang

rationale: Confirmed by jyang at Phase-5 start. Degenerate kappa (JD-4): at zero chance-corrected information (1-pe or den ~ 0, e.g. all-A/all-A x20) cohens_kappa (judge/calibrate.py) and weighted_kappa (review/kappa.py) return undefined (None) and callers set sufficient=False even when n>=min_human_verdicts, instead of 1.0/sufficient which over-credits the judge exactly when the data cannot support any kappa. Applies to BOTH kappa families so judge outcome kappa and process per-dimension quadratic-weighted kappa do not diverge. ClassCalibration/DimensionCalibration.kappa are already Optional. Implementation: Phase 5 slice 5H.
author: jyang
